author: Nadirs
text:
Rewrite skeleton in place: new crate root, legacy impl deleted

Supersedes bl-505f's src/next/ alongside-quarantine. The quarantine protected an invariant that does not exist: this is a dev repo, the system-installed bl is the self-hoster, so the repo's main need not build a working bl during the rewrite. Dropping the pretense removes a next:: prefix from 16 phases and a dead parallel copy of the old code.

All ~140 legacy modules and the old tests/ tree are deleted (recoverable from git history; old behavior is reference, not load-bearing — the migration in §17 reads the balls/tasks state branch, not src/). The greenfield now IS the crate: src/lib.rs + src/main.rs at root, bl dispatching the §8 verb->op entrypoint.

Content is the §0/§8 spine, no behavior: Verb (§9, 14 verbs) -> OpClass (mutating vs diffless = the §8 seal/no-seal split) -> Op::phases (five-step lifecycle for mutating ops, pre+post for diffless). balls::run is the dispatch; the bl binary is a thin shell over it and is actually runnable. Cargo.toml deps trimmed to std-only; phases re-add deps as the architecture needs them.

Harness: tests/dispatch.rs builds bl and execs it from a throwaway TempDir, never the dev repo — the seed for later phases to git-init a /tmp balls repo and assert real behavior. Coverage 100% (48/48, incl. main.rs via the subprocess exec); all files well under the 300-line cap. Do not make install from this tree until cutover.
